DESCRIPTION: Required tables are not present
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.01 (Table IID): checks whether all 25 PCORnet CDM tables exist in <DB_PARAM>.<SCHEMA_NAME>. Core required: DEMOGRAPHIC, ENROLLMENT, ENCOUNTER, DIAGNOSIS, PROCEDURES, HARVEST. EHR-only (LAB_RESULT_CM, PRESCRIBING, VITAL) flagged as exceptions only when EHR is inferred from HARVEST. Remaining CDM tables checked informationally.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.01', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE| 'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_01(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL').
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.01.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE: MISSING_REQUIRED_TABLE_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) means the required table is missing.

DESCRIPTION: Required tables are not populated. DEMOGRAPHIC, ENROLLMENT, ENCOUNTER, DIAGNOSIS, PROCEDURES, and HARVEST are required for all network partners; LAB_RESULT_CM, PRESCRIBING, and VITAL are required for network partners with electronic health record data
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.02 (Table IID): required CDM tables are populated (rowcount > 0). Uses INFORMATION_SCHEMA row_count when available; falls back to COUNT(*) if needed.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.02', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_02(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL').
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.02.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE: EMPTY_REQUIRED_TABLE_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) means table exists but is empty. MISSING_REQUIRED_TABLE_FLAG=1 means table missing entirely.

DESCRIPTION: Required fields are not present
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.03 (Table IID): required columns exist in each CDM table. Expected columns come from CHARACTERIZATION.EDC_REF.REQUIRED_STRUCTURE_RAW and are compared to <DB_PARAM>.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.03', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_03(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL').
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.03.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE: MISSING_REQUIRED_COLUMNS_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) means at least one required column is missing; DETAILS.missing_column_list contains examples.

DESCRIPTION: Required fields do not conform to data model specifications for data type, length, or name
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.04 (Table IID): required columns conform to expected type/length (v1). R_TYPE=2 expects string types and enforces CHARACTER_MAXIMUM_LENGTH >= R_LENGTH when provided. R_TYPE=1 expects non-string types; *_TIME columns may be TIME or short strings.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.04', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_04(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL').
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.04.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE: ANY_CONFORMANCE_ISSUE_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates at least one type/length mismatch; DETAILS.examples contains sample mismatches.

DESCRIPTION: Tables have primary key definition errors
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.05 (Table IIA): primary key definition errors. Evaluates declared primary key uniqueness per CDM table by computing PK_NON_NULL_COUNT vs PK_DISTINCT_COUNT and flags duplicates.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.05', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_05(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be a single CDM table name or ALL.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.05. Emits metrics per SOURCE_TABLE: ROW_COUNT, PK_NON_NULL_COUNT, PK_DISTINCT_COUNT, PK_DUPLICATE_ROW_COUNT, PK_DUPLICATE_FLAG.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE: PK_DUPLICATE_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates duplicate primary keys among rows where all PK components are non-null (PK_DUPLICATE_ROW_COUNT > 0). DETAILS.pk_columns shows the PK components used.

DESCRIPTION: Required fields contain values outside of data model specifications
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.06: required fields contain values outside CDM value set specifications (VALUESETS). Flags when any out-of-spec values exist (pct > 0).
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.06', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_06(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL or a specific CDM table name.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.06. EDC_TABLE and SOURCE_TABLE are the evaluated table; CODE_TYPE is the evaluated field name.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: For each table+field with a CDM value set: NON_MISSING_N counts non-null/non-blank rows. OUT_OF_SPEC_N counts those not in the CDM allowed list (case-insensitive). OUT_OF_SPEC_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates out-of-spec percent > 0%.

DESCRIPTION: Required fields have non-permissible missing values
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.07 (Table IIC): non-permissible missing values in required (not null) fields. Flags when any missing values exist.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.07', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_07(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL or a specific CDM table name.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.07. EDC_TABLE and SOURCE_TABLE are the evaluated table; CODE_TYPE is the evaluated field name.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: For each required-not-null field: MISSING_N counts NULL values (and for strings: also blanks). MISSING_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates MISSING_N > 0.

DESCRIPTION: Tables contain orphan PATIDs (PATIDS that are not present in the DEMOGRAPHIC table)
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.08 (Table IIE): orphan PATIDs. Flags PATIDs present in CDM tables but not present in DEMOGRAPHIC.PATID.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.08', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_08(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be a CDM table name or ALL.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.08. CODE_TYPE indicates which PATID field was evaluated (PATID, PATID_1, PATID_2). Metrics include TABLE_PATID_DISTINCT_N, ORPHAN_PATID_DISTINCT_N, ORPHAN_PATID_PCT, ORPHAN_PATID_FLAG.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE + CODE_TYPE: ORPHAN_PATID_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates at least one distinct orphan PATID exists (i.e., not found in DEMOGRAPHIC).

DESCRIPTION: Tables contain orphan ENCOUNTERIDs (ENCOUNTERIDs that are not present in the ENCOUNTER table) for more than 5% of records
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.09 (Table IIE): orphan ENCOUNTERIDs. Flags ENCOUNTERIDs present in other CDM tables but not present in ENCOUNTER.ENCOUNTERID.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.09', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_09(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be a CDM table name or ALL.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.09. Metrics per SOURCE_TABLE: TABLE_ENCOUNTERID_DISTINCT_N, ORPHAN_ENCOUNTERID_DISTINCT_N, ORPHAN_ENCOUNTERID_PCT, ORPHAN_ENCOUNTERID_FLAG.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE: ORPHAN_ENCOUNTERID_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates orphan ENCOUNTERIDs (not found in ENCOUNTER) represent 5% or more of distinct ENCOUNTERIDs in that table.

DESCRIPTION: Replication errors between the ENCOUNTER, PROCEDURES and DIAGNOSIS tables. Replication errors are ENCOUNTERIDs in the DIAGNOSIS or PROCEDURES table where the encounter type or admit date does not match the corresponding value in the ENCOUNTER table
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.10 (Table IIE): replication errors between ENCOUNTER and DIAGNOSIS/PROCEDURES. Flags records where ENC_TYPE and/or ADMIT_DATE do not match the corresponding ENCOUNTER record for the same ENCOUNTERID.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.10', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_10(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be DIAGNOSIS, PROCEDURES, or ALL.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.10. Metrics are produced per SOURCE_TABLE (DIAGNOSIS/PROCEDURES).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE: REPLICATION_ERROR_RECORDS counts records with mismatched ENC_TYPE and/or ADMIT_DATE compared to ENCOUNTER. REPLICATION_ERROR_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates replication errors exist. Use ENC_TYPE_MISMATCH_RECORDS and ADMIT_DATE_MISMATCH_RECORDS to see which attribute drives mismatches.

DESCRIPTION: More than 5% of encounters are assigned to more than one patient
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.11 (Table IIE): encounters assigned to more than one patient. Flags ENCOUNTERIDs in ENCOUNTER with >1 distinct PATID.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.11', 'part1', 'ENCOUNTER'|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_11(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, 'ENCOUNTER'|'ALL').
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.11. Metrics: ENCOUNTERID_DISTINCT_N, MULTI_PATID_ENCOUNTERID_N, MULTI_PATID_ENCOUNTERID_PCT, MULTI_PATID_ENCOUNTERID_FLAG.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: MULTI_PATID_ENCOUNTERID_PCT is the percent of distinct ENCOUNTERIDs associated with >1 PATID. MULTI_PATID_ENCOUNTERID_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates pct > 5%%.

DESCRIPTION: Tables contain orphan PROVIDERIDs (PROVIDERIDs that are not present in the PROVIDER table)
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.12 (Table IIE): orphan PROVIDERIDs. Flags provider identifiers referenced in other CDM tables that are not present in PROVIDER.PROVIDERID.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.12', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_12(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be a CDM table name or ALL.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.12. CODE_TYPE indicates which provider-id field was evaluated (e.g., PROVIDERID, RX_PROVIDERID, VX_PROVIDERID, MEDADMIN_PROVIDERID). Metrics include TABLE_PROVIDERID_DISTINCT_N, ORPHAN_PROVIDERID_DISTINCT_N, ORPHAN_PROVIDERID_PCT, ORPHAN_PROVIDERID_FLAG.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE + CODE_TYPE: ORPHAN_PROVIDERID_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates at least one distinct orphan provider ID exists (i.e., not found in PROVIDER). Rows with METRIC='STATUS' indicate the table/column was skipped or errored.

DESCRIPTION: More than 5% of CPT�/HCPCS, CVX, ICD, NDC, LOINC or RXNORM codes do not conform to the expected length or content based on terminology-specific heuristics
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.13 (Table IIF): terminology heuristics / potential code errors. Wraps CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.POTENTIAL_CODE_ERRORS(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, TABLE_LIST) and ingests <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION.CODE_SUMMARY.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.13', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_13(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be a domain table (e.g., DIAGNOSIS) or ALL.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.13.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE+CODE_TYPE: metrics include TOTAL_RECORDS/TOTAL_CODES/BAD_RECORDS/BAD_CODES/BAD_RECORD_PCT. EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE when BAD_RECORD_PCT > 5.

DESCRIPTION: Patients in the DEMOGRAPHIC table are not in the HASH_TOKEN table
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.14 (Table IIE, investigative): patients in DEMOGRAPHIC are not in HASH_TOKEN. Compares DEMOGRAPHIC.PATID to HASH_TOKEN.PATID.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.14', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_14(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL, DEMOGRAPHIC, or HASH_TOKEN.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.14. Metrics: DEMOGRAPHIC_PATID_DISTINCT_N, HASH_TOKEN_PATID_DISTINCT_N, MISSING_HASH_TOKEN_PATID_DISTINCT_N, MISSING_HASH_TOKEN_PATID_PCT, MISSING_HASH_TOKEN_PATID_FLAG.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: MISSING_HASH_TOKEN_PATID_DISTINCT_N counts distinct PATIDs present in DEMOGRAPHIC but absent from HASH_TOKEN. MISSING_HASH_TOKEN_PATID_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates missing PATIDs exist (investigative).

DESCRIPTION: Fields with undefined lengths that are present in more than one table (PATID, ENCOUNTERID, PRESCRIBINGID, PROCEDURESID, PROVIDERID, MEDADMIN_PROVIDERID, OBSGEN_PROVIDERID, OBSCLIN_PROVIDERID, RX_PROVIDERID, and VX_PROVIDERID) do not have harmonized field lengths
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.15 (Table IID): fields with undefined lengths that are present across multiple tables do not have harmonized field lengths. Flags when a harmonization group has >1 distinct declared max length.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.15', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_15(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL or a specific CDM table name.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.15. SOURCE_TABLE is INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS; CODE_TYPE is the harmonization group (e.g., PATID_FAMILY, ENCOUNTERID, PROVIDERID_FAMILY).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: DISTINCT_LENGTH_N counts distinct declared max lengths across all occurrences of the harmonization group. HARMONIZED_LENGTH_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates DISTINCT_LENGTH_N > 1.

DESCRIPTION: Laboratory results or clinical observations are recorded in the wrong table based on the LOINC� classtype
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.16 (Table IIF): misplaced LOINC codes based on LOINC CLASSTYPE. Joins to CHARACTERIZATION.EDC_REF.LOINC_REF_RAW. LAB_RESULT_CM and LAB_HISTORY expect CLASSTYPE=1; OBS_CLIN expects CLASSTYPE=2; OBS_GEN should not use CLASSTYPE=1.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.16', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_16(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be LAB_RESULT_CM, LAB_HISTORY, OBS_CLIN, OBS_GEN, or ALL.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.16.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE: metrics include RECORDS/MISPLACED_RECORDS/MISPLACED_CODES/MISPLACED_RECORD_PCT. EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE when MISPLACED_RECORDS > 0.

DESCRIPTION: Zip codes in the ENCOUNTER or LDS_ADDRESS_HISTORY table have non-conforming values (alphabetical characters or the wrong number of digits)
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.17 (Table IID): zip codes in ENCOUNTER.FACILITY_LOCATION or LDS_ADDRESS_HISTORY.ADDRESS_ZIP5/ADDRESS_ZIP9 do not conform to expected values. Flags any non-missing value that contains alpha characters or does not have the expected number of digits.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.17', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_17(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL, ENCOUNTER, or LDS_ADDRESS_HISTORY.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.17. EDC_TABLE is the evaluated table; SOURCE_TABLE is the evaluated table; CODE_TYPE is the field name.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: BAD_ZIP_N counts non-missing values that contain alphabetic characters or do not match the expected digit count (ZIP5=5 digits, ZIP9=9 digits). BAD_ZIP_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates BAD_ZIP_N > 0.

DESCRIPTION: Table refresh dates are not documented in the HARVEST table for populated tables
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.18 (Table ID): populated CDM tables have refresh dates documented in HARVEST (REFRESH_* fields). Computes NOBS and whether the corresponding HARVEST refresh field is present.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.18', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_18(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be a CDM table name, HARVEST, or ALL.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.18.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE: MISSING_REFRESH_DATE_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) means table is populated (NOBS>0) but the HARVEST refresh date is missing; DETAILS.harvest_column and DETAILS.refresh_value show the source field/value.

DESCRIPTION: More than 10% of the values in any hash token field are assigned to multiple patients
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.19 (Table IIE, investigative): hash token duplication. Flags token fields where >10% of distinct valid token values are assigned to multiple patients.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.19', 'part1', 'HASH_TOKEN'|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_19(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, 'HASH_TOKEN'|'ALL').
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.19. CODE_TYPE is the token field name (TOKEN_*). Metrics include VALID_N, VALID_DISTINCT_N, VALID_MULTI_PATID_TOKEN_N, VALID_MULTI_PATID_TOKEN_PCT, TOKEN_MULTI_PATID_FLAG.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per token field (CODE_TYPE): VALID_MULTI_PATID_TOKEN_PCT is the percent of distinct valid token values assigned to >1 PATID. TOKEN_MULTI_PATID_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates pct > 10%%.

DESCRIPTION: More than 5% of LOINC records in the LAB_RESULT_CM, PRO_CM, and OBS_CLIN tables are panel codes based on the LOINC� panel type
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.20 (Table IIG): percent of LOINC records that are panels. Flags when >5% of LOINC-coded records map to PANEL_TYPE='Panel'.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.20', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_20(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL, LAB_RESULT_CM, OBS_CLIN, or PRO_CM.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.20. CODE_TYPE is the code column (LAB_LOINC, OBSCLIN_CODE, PRO_CODE).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: PANEL_PCT = PANEL_N / LOINC_RECORD_N * 100. PANEL_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates percent > 5%.

DESCRIPTION: STATE_FIPS, COUNTY_FIPS and RUCA_ZIP fields have non-conforming values (alphabetical characters or discrepancies between STATE_FIPS and COUNTY_FIPS)
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 1.21 (Table IID): geo codes in LDS_ADDRESS_HISTORY do not conform to expected values. Flags any alphabetic characters, incorrect digit counts, or COUNTY_FIPS state mismatch.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '1.21', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_1_21(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL or LDS_ADDRESS_HISTORY.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=1.21. CODE_TYPE indicates the field (STATE_FIPS, COUNTY_FIPS, RUCA_ZIP) or ALL for summary metrics.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: BAD_* metrics count bad non-missing values. GEO_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates any geo code violations exist (TOTAL_BAD_N > 0).

DESCRIPTION: More than 5% of records have future dates. Future dates are defined as those with dates occurring after the maximum refresh date in the HARVEST table
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 2.01 (Table IIIA): future dates. Flags date fields with values after the maximum refresh date derived from HARVEST refresh_*_date fields.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '2.01', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_2_01(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be a supported CDM table name or ALL.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=2.01. SOURCE_TABLE is the CDM table; CODE_TYPE is the date column name. MAX_REFRESH_DATE is stored under SOURCE_TABLE='HARVEST'.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE+CODE_TYPE: FUTURE_DATE_N counts records with date > MAX_REFRESH_DATE (denominator DATE_POPULATED_N). FUTURE_DATE_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates FUTURE_DATE_PCT > 5%%.

DESCRIPTION: More than 10% of records fall into the lowest or highest categories of age (<0 or >89), height (<21 inches or >76 inches), weight (<0 lbs or >350 lbs), diastolic blood pressure (<40 mmHg or >120 mmHg), systolic blood pressure (<40 mmHg or >210 mmHg), or dispensed days supply (<1 day or >90 days)
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 2.02 (Table IIIB): records with extreme values. Calculates percent of evaluated records in low/high extreme ranges for VITAL measures (HT/WT/DIASTOLIC/SYSTOLIC/BMI) and DISPENSING days supply (DISPENSE_SUP).
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '2.02', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_2_02(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be VITAL, DISPENSING, or ALL.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=2.02. SOURCE_TABLE is VITAL or DISPENSING; CODE_TYPE is the field name (e.g., HT, DISPENSE_SUP).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE+CODE_TYPE: EXTREME_N = LOW_EXTREME_N + HIGH_EXTREME_N; EXTREME_PCT = EXTREME_N / RECORDS_EVAL_N * 100. EXTREME_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates EXTREME_PCT > 10%%.

DESCRIPTION: More than 5% of patients have illogical date relationships. Illogical date relationships are defined as dates of service which occur before a patient's birth date or after a patient's death date; procedure dates occurring more than 5 days before the admit date or 5 days after the discharge date for the same encounter; or stop dates before start dates in the EXTERNAL_MEDS, MED_ADMIN, OBS_CLIN, OBS_GEN, or PAT_RELATIONSHIP tables
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 2.03 (Table IIIC): illogical date relationships. Flags (a) service dates before birth, (b) service dates after death, (c) procedure PX_DATE outside encounter admit/discharge window (+/- 5 days), and (d) stop dates before start dates in EXTERNAL_MEDS/MED_ADMIN/OBS_CLIN/OBS_GEN/PAT_RELATIONSHIP.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '2.03', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_2_03(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be a supported CDM table name or ALL.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=2.03. SOURCE_TABLE is the CDM table. CODE_TYPE encodes the rule and date field(s) (e.g., BEFORE_BIRTH:ADMIT_DATE, AFTER_DEATH:DX_DATE, PXDATE_OUTSIDE_ENCOUNTER, STOP_BEFORE_START:RX_START_DATE:RX_END_DATE).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Per SOURCE_TABLE+CODE_TYPE: ILLOGICAL_N counts rows violating the rule; denominator RECORDS_EVAL_N depends on available dates (and birth/death where applicable). ILLOGICAL_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates ILLOGICAL_PCT > 5%%.

DESCRIPTION: The average number of encounters per visit is > 2.0 for inpatient (IP), emergency department (ED), or ED to inpatient (EI) encounters. A visit is defined as a unique combination of PATID, ENC_TYPE, ADMIT_DATE, and PROVIDERID in the ENCOUNTER table
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 2.04 (Table IIID): average number of encounters per visit > 2.0 for IP/ED/EI. A visit is defined as PATID+ENC_TYPE+ADMIT_DATE+PROVIDERID in ENCOUNTER.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '2.04', 'part2', 'ENCOUNTER'|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_2_04(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, 'ENCOUNTER'|'ALL').
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=2.04. SOURCE_TABLE is ENCOUNTER; CODE_TYPE is ENC_TYPE (IP/ED/EI).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: ENCOUNTERS_PER_VISIT_AVG = ENCOUNTER_RECORDS_N / VISIT_DISTINCT_N per ENC_TYPE (IP/ED/EI). A visit is defined as PATID+ENC_TYPE+ADMIT_DATE+PROVIDERID in ENCOUNTER. Rows with missing PATID, ADMIT_DATE, or PROVIDERID are excluded from the visit calculation; DETAILS.total_rows_n / DETAILS.eligible_rows_n / DETAILS.excluded_rows_n show the counts. ENCOUNTERS_PER_VISIT_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates average > 2.0.

DESCRIPTION: The average number of principal diagnoses per known DX_ORIGIN per encounter is above threshold [2.0 for inpatient (IP) and ED to inpatient (EI))
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 2.07 (Table IVE): principal diagnoses per encounter with any principal diagnosis. Flags when rate > 2.0 for IP/EI by DX_ORIGIN.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '2.07', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_2_07(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL, ENCOUNTER, or DIAGNOSIS.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=2.07. CODE_TYPE is DX_ORIGIN (known values only).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: For each DX_ORIGIN (IP/EI only): rate = PRINCIPAL_DX_RECORDS_N / ENC_WITH_ANY_PRINCIPAL_DX_N. PRINCIPAL_DX_PER_ENC_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates rate > 2.0.

DESCRIPTION: The monthly volume of encounter, diagnosis, procedure, vital, prescribing, medication administration, external medications, laboratory records, or patient-reported outcomes is an outlier. Outliers are defined as months with 0 records or a significant decrease compared to the average volume in the previous 12 months. Encounters, diagnoses, and procedures are limited to ambulatory (AV), telehealth (TH), emergency department (ED), ED to inpatient (EI), or inpatient (IP) settings.
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 2.08 (Table IIIG): monthly volume outliers. Flags months where volume is 0 or more than 7 SD below the lagged 12-month rolling mean (after applying eligibility/exclusion rules).
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '2.08', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_2_08(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL or a specific CDM table name.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=2.08. MONTHLY_OUTLIER rows have EDC_TABLE=table, CODE_TYPE=category (ENC_TYPE or 'n/a'), VALUE_STR=YYYY-MM, and DETAILS includes lag_avg/lag_std/dsd.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: OUTLIER_FOUND_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates at least one monthly outlier was detected. For each outlier, DETAILS.outlier_reason indicates whether volume was zero or z-score < -7.

DESCRIPTION: Less than 80% of patients with a face-to-face encounter during the past 5 years have at least 1 face-to-face diagnosis and 1 vital measurement. Face-to-face is defined as an encounter type of ambulatory visit (AV), emergency department (ED), emergency department admit to inpatient hospital stay (EI), inpatient hospital (IP), or observation stay (OS).
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 2.09 (Table IB): percentage of patients with encounters in the most recent 5 years. Flags when <80%. Anchor date is max ENCOUNTER.ADMIT_DATE.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '2.09', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_2_09(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL, DEMOGRAPHIC, or ENCOUNTER.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=2.09. Metrics are single-row summary values for the datamart.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: PCT_PATID_WITH_ENC_5YR = (# distinct PATID in ENCOUNTER with ADMIT_DATE in [anchor-5y, anchor]) / (# distinct PATID in DEMOGRAPHIC) * 100. Flag=1 indicates percent < 80%.

DESCRIPTION: The average number of diagnoses records with known diagnosis types per encounter is below threshold [1.0 for ambulatory (AV), inpatient (IP), emergency department (ED), ED to inpatient (EI), or telehealth (TH) encounters].
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.01 (Table IVA): diagnosis completeness by encounter. Flags when average number of DIAGNOSIS records with known DX_TYPE per encounter is below threshold (1.0) for AV/IP/ED/EI/TH.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.01', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_01(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL, ENCOUNTER, or DIAGNOSIS.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.01. SOURCE_TABLE is DIAGNOSIS; CODE_TYPE is ENC_TYPE (AV/IP/ED/EI/TH).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: AVG_DIAG_PER_ENCOUNTER = DIAG_RECORDS_KNOWN_DXTYPE_N / ENCOUNTER_DISTINCT_N per ENC_TYPE, where numerator includes only DIAGNOSIS rows with DX_TYPE not in (NI, UN, OT, blank). AVG_BELOW_THRESHOLD_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates average < 1.0.

DESCRIPTION: The average number of procedure records with known procedure types per encounter is below threshold [0.75 for ambulatory (AV) encounters, 0.75 for emergency department (ED) encounters, 1.00 for ED to inpatient (EI) encounters, and 1.00 for inpatient (IP) encounters].
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.02 (Table IVB): procedures completeness by encounter. Flags when average number of PROCEDURES records with known PX_TYPE per encounter is below threshold (AV<0.75, ED<0.75, EI<1.0, IP<1.0).
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.02', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_02(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL, ENCOUNTER, or PROCEDURES.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.02. SOURCE_TABLE is PROCEDURES; CODE_TYPE is ENC_TYPE (AV/ED/EI/IP/TH).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: AVG_PX_PER_ENCOUNTER = PX_RECORDS_KNOWN_PXTYPE_N / ENCOUNTER_DISTINCT_N per ENC_TYPE, where numerator includes only PROCEDURES rows with PX_TYPE not in (NI, UN, OT, blank). AVG_BELOW_THRESHOLD_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates average below the ENC_TYPE-specific threshold.

DESCRIPTION: More than 10% of records have missing or unknown values for the following fields:  DISCHARGE_DISPOSITION (IP/EI encounters only), DISPENSE_SUP, DX_SOURCE, SEX, code fields [DEATH_CAUSE_CODE, MEDADMIN_CODE, OBSCLIN_CODE, OBSGEN_CODE], code type fields [DX_TYPE, CONDITION_TYPE, MEDADMIN_TYPE, OBSCLIN_TYPE, OBSGEN_TYPE, PX_TYPE, VX_CODE_TYPE], date fields [BIRTH_DATE, DISCHARGE_DATE (IP/EI encounters only), EXT_RECORD_DATE, RX_ORDER_DATE, PX_DATE, VX_RECORD_DATE],  ENCOUNTERID fields in selected tables [DIAGNOSIS, LAB_RESULT_CM, PRESCRIBING, MED_ADMIN, OBS_CLIN, PROCEDURES, and VITAL], and provenance fields [CONDITION_SOURCE, DEATH_CAUSE_SOURCE, DEATH_SOURCE, DISPENSE_SOURCE, DX_ORIGIN,  EXTMED_SOURCE, EXT_BASIS, MEDADMIN_SOURCE, LAB_RESULT_SOURCE, PX_SOURCE,  RX_SOURCE, VITAL_SOURCE,  VX_SOURCE]
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.03: CDM conformance for required (not null) fields. Flags any non-zero missing percent.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.03', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_03(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL or a specific CDM table name.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.03. EDC_TABLE and SOURCE_TABLE are the evaluated table; CODE_TYPE is the evaluated column name.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: For each evaluated table+column: DENOM_N counts rows; MISSING_N counts rows with NULL (and for string fields: also blank). MISSING_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates missing percent > 0%.

DESCRIPTION: See Table IVC
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.03: CDM conformance for required (not null) fields. Flags any non-zero missing percent.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.03', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_03(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL or a specific CDM table name.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.03. EDC_TABLE and SOURCE_TABLE are the evaluated table; CODE_TYPE is the evaluated column name.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: For each evaluated table+column: DENOM_N counts rows; MISSING_N counts rows with NULL (and for string fields: also blank). MISSING_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates missing percent > 0%.

DESCRIPTION: Less than 50% of patients with encounters have DIAGNOSIS records
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.04 (Table IB, required): diagnosis completeness by patient. Flags when <50% of patients with encounters have DIAGNOSIS records.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.04', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_04(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL, ENCOUNTER, or DIAGNOSIS.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.04. SOURCE_TABLE is DIAGNOSIS; CODE_TYPE is PATID_COVERAGE. Metrics include ENCOUNTER_PATID_DISTINCT_N, DIAGNOSIS_PATID_DISTINCT_N, DIAGNOSIS_COVERAGE_PCT, DIAGNOSIS_COVERAGE_FLAG.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: DIAGNOSIS_COVERAGE_PCT = DIAGNOSIS_PATID_DISTINCT_N / ENCOUNTER_PATID_DISTINCT_N * 100. DIAGNOSIS_COVERAGE_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates coverage < 50%.

DESCRIPTION: Less than 50% of patients with encounters have PROCEDURES records
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.05 (Table IB, required): procedures completeness by patient. Flags when <50% of patients with encounters have PROCEDURES records.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.05', 'part1', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_05(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL, ENCOUNTER, or PROCEDURES.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.05. SOURCE_TABLE is PROCEDURES; CODE_TYPE is PATID_COVERAGE. Metrics include ENCOUNTER_PATID_DISTINCT_N, PROCEDURES_PATID_DISTINCT_N, PROCEDURES_COVERAGE_PCT, PROCEDURES_COVERAGE_FLAG.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: PROCEDURES_COVERAGE_PCT = PROCEDURES_PATID_DISTINCT_N / ENCOUNTER_PATID_DISTINCT_N * 100. PROCEDURES_COVERAGE_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates coverage < 50%.

DESCRIPTION: More than 10% of IP (inpatient) or ED to inpatient (EI) encounters with any diagnosis from a known DX_ORIGIN don't have a principal diagnosis from that source
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.06 (Table IVE): principal diagnosis completeness by DX_ORIGIN for IP/EI. Flags when >10% of IP/EI encounters with any diagnosis from a known DX_ORIGIN lack a principal diagnosis (PDX='P') from that same origin.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.06', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_06(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL, DIAGNOSIS, or ENCOUNTER.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.06. SOURCE_TABLE is DIAGNOSIS; CODE_TYPE is DX_ORIGIN (known values only).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: For each DX_ORIGIN: denominator ENCOUNTERS_WITH_KNOWN_ORIGIN_N counts distinct ENCOUNTERID+DX_ORIGIN combos (IP/EI only). Numerator ENCOUNTERS_MISSING_PDX_FOR_ORIGIN_N counts those with no DIAGNOSIS row where PDX='P'. MISSING_PDX_FOR_ORIGIN_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates percent > 10%.

DESCRIPTION: Encounters, diagnoses, or procedures in an ambulatory (AV), telehealth (TH), emergency department (ED), ED to inpatient (EI), or inpatient (IP) setting are less than 75% complete two months prior to the current month. Data completeness is calculated by comparing actual volume to the average volume during the previous year
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.07 (Table IVF): significant decreases in encounter/diagnosis/procedure monthly volumes. Flags Month -2 if count=0 or <75% of benchmark average (benchmark = average of months -12..-23). Anchor date is CURRENT_DATE at run time.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.07', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_07(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL, ENCOUNTER, DIAGNOSIS, or PROCEDURES.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.07. EDC_TABLE indicates series (ENCOUNTER/DIAGNOSIS/PROCEDURES) and summary rows use EDC_TABLE='ALL'.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: DECREASE_FLAG=1 indicates that series' Month -2 is 0 or <75% of benchmark average. ANY_DECREASE_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates any of the three series triggered.

DESCRIPTION: Less than 80% of prescribing orders are mapped to a RXCUI which fully specifies the ingredient, strength and dose form (i.e. RXCUI codes that have a Term Type of SCD, SBD,BPCK, or GPCK)
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.08 (Table IVH): RxNorm term type tier 1 mapping completeness for PRESCRIBING. Flags when Tier 1 percent < 80% or Tier 1 numerator is 0.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.08', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_08(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL or PRESCRIBING.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.08. CODE_TYPE indicates tier (TIER_1, TIER_1_BRAND).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Tier metrics are emitted by CODE_TYPE: TIER_1..TIER_5 and TIER_1_BRAND. RECORDS_N/RECORD_PCT describe the tier distribution across PRESCRIBING records. TIER1_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates Tier 1 percent < 80% or zero Tier 1 records.

DESCRIPTION: Less than 80% of laboratory results are mapped to LAB_LOINC and have either a quantitative result (RESULT_NUM is not null and RESULT_MODIFIER is not NI, UN, OT, or null) or a qualitative result (RESULT_QUAL is not NI, UN, OT, or null)
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.09 (Table IVI): lab result completeness. Flags when <80% of LAB_RESULT_CM records are mapped to LAB_LOINC and have either a quantitative result (RESULT_NUM present and RESULT_MODIFIER not in NI/UN/OT) or a qualitative result (RESULT_QUAL not in NI/UN/OT).
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.09', 'part2', 'LAB_RESULT_CM'|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_09(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, 'LAB_RESULT_CM'|'ALL').
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.09. SOURCE_TABLE is LAB_RESULT_CM. CODE_TYPE corresponds to CHARACTERIZATION.EDC_REF.TBL_IVI_REF_RAW.SOURCE_TABLE_ROW (KNOWN_TEST, KNOWN_TEST_RESULT).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: CODE_TYPE corresponds to TBL_IVI_REF_RAW.SOURCE_TABLE_ROW. DC 3.09 exception is evaluated on CODE_TYPE='KNOWN_TEST_RESULT': CATEGORY_PCT < 80% => CATEGORY_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE). Other CODE_TYPEs (KNOWN_TEST_RESULT_NUM, KNOWN_TEST_RESULT_NUM_UNIT, KNOWN_TEST_NUM_RESULT_RANGE) are emitted for reporting and for alignment with IVI reference logic.

DESCRIPTION: Less than 80% of quantitative results for tests mapped to LAB_LOINC fully specify the normal range in the  range and modifier fields
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.10 (Table IVI): normal range completeness for quantitative lab results. Flags when <80% of quantitative LAB_RESULT_CM results mapped to LAB_LOINC fully specify the normal range in NORM_RANGE_* and NORM_MODIFIER_* fields.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.10', 'part2', 'LAB_RESULT_CM'|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_10(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, 'LAB_RESULT_CM'|'ALL').
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.10. SOURCE_TABLE is LAB_RESULT_CM; CODE_TYPE is KNOWN_TEST_NUM_RESULT_RANGE.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: FULL_RANGE_PCT_OF_QUANT = NUM_FULL_RANGE_N / DENOM_QUANT_MAPPED_N * 100. FULL_RANGE_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates percent < 80%. DETAILS includes the criteria wording from TBL_IVI_REF_RAW.

DESCRIPTION: Vital, prescribing, or laboratory records are less than 75% complete three months prior to the current month. Data completeness is calculated by comparing actual volume to the average volume during the previous year
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.11 (Table IVG): significant decreases in vitals/prescribing/lab monthly volumes. Flags Month -3 if count=0 or <75% of benchmark average (benchmark = average of months -12..-23). Anchor date is CURRENT_DATE at run time.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.11', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_11(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL or a specific CDM table name.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.11. EDC_TABLE indicates series (VITAL/PRESCRIBING/LAB_RESULT_CM) and summary rows use EDC_TABLE='ALL'.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: DECREASE_FLAG=1 indicates that series' Month -3 is 0 or <75% of benchmark average. ANY_DECREASE_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates any of the three series triggered.

DESCRIPTION: Less than 80% of quantitative results for tests mapped to LAB_LOINC fully specify the RESULT_UNIT (i.e. RESULT_NUM is not null and RESULT_UNIT is not NI, UN, OT, or null)
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.12 (Table IVI): quantitative lab results specify RESULT_UNIT. Flags when <80% of quantitative known tests have a unit.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.12', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_12(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL or LAB_RESULT_CM.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.12. CODE_TYPE is KNOWN_TEST_RESULT_NUM_UNIT.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: PCT_WITH_UNIT = NUM_WITH_UNIT_N / DENOM_QUANT_KNOWN_N * 100. WITH_UNIT_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates percent < 80% (when denom > 0).

DESCRIPTION: Medication administration, dispensing, or clinical observation records are less than 75% complete three months prior to the current month. Data completeness is calculated by comparing actual volume to the average volume during the previous year
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.14 (Table IVJ): medication administration, dispensing, and clinical observation monthly volumes. Flags Month -3 if count=0 or <75% of benchmark average (benchmark = average of months -12..-23). Anchor date is CURRENT_DATE at run time.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.14', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_14(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL, MED_ADMIN, DISPENSING, or OBS_CLIN.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.14. EDC_TABLE indicates series (MED_ADMIN/DISPENSING/OBS_CLIN) and summary rows use EDC_TABLE='ALL'.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: DECREASE_FLAG=1 indicates that series' Month -3 is 0 or <75% of benchmark average. ANY_DECREASE_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates any of the three series triggered.

DESCRIPTION: Less than 80% of medication administrations mapped to RXNORM are mapped to a RXCUI that fully specifies the ingredient, strength and dose form (i.e. RXCUI codes that have a Term Type of SCD, SBD,BPCK, or GPCK)
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.15 (Table IVH): RxNorm term type tier 1 mapping completeness for MED_ADMIN (MEDADMIN_TYPE='RX'). Flags when Tier 1 percent < 80% or Tier 1 numerator is 0.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.15', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_15(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL or MED_ADMIN.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.15. CODE_TYPE indicates tier (TIER_1..TIER_5).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: Tier metrics are emitted by CODE_TYPE: TIER_1..TIER_5. RECORDS_N/RECORD_PCT describe the tier distribution across all MED_ADMIN records, where MEDADMIN_TYPE='RX' rows use MEDADMIN_CODE as RxCUI and other types are treated as Tier 5. TIER1_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates Tier 1 percent < 80% or zero Tier 1 records. If there are 0 MED_ADMIN records, the check is not applicable and TIER1_FLAG=0.

DESCRIPTION: Less than 80% of clinical observations are mapped to an OBSCLIN_CODE and have a quantitative result (OBSCLIN_RESULT_NUM is not null and OBSCLIN_RESULT_MODIFIER is not NI,UN,OT or null), qualitative result (OBSCLIN_RESULT_QUAL is not NI,UN,OT or null) or narrative result (OBSCLIN_RESULT_TEXT is not null)
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.16 (Table IVI): clinical observations are mapped to a known OBSCLIN_CODE and have a known result (quantitative/qualitative/narrative). Flags when percent <80% or numerator is 0.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.16', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_16(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL or OBS_CLIN.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.16. CODE_TYPE is KNOWN_OBSCLIN_CODE_WITH_KNOWN_RESULT.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: PCT_KNOWN_CODE_WITH_KNOWN_RESULT = NUM_KNOWN_CODE_WITH_KNOWN_RESULT_N / DENOM_RECORDS_N * 100. KNOWN_RESULT_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates percent < 80% or numerator=0 (when denom > 0).

DESCRIPTION: Less than 80% of quantitative results for tests mapped to OBSCLIN_CODE fully specify the RESULT_UNIT (i.e. OBSCLIN_RESULT_NUM is not null and OBSCLIN_RESULT_UNIT is not NI, UN, OT, or null)
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 3.17 (Table IVI): quantitative clinical observations specify OBSCLIN_RESULT_UNIT for known tests (OBSCLIN_TYPE='LC' and OBSCLIN_CODE in LOINC reference). Flags when <80% have a unit or numerator is 0 (when denom > 0).
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('CHAR_BND', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '3.17', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_3_17(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL'). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL or OBS_CLIN.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=3.17. CODE_TYPE is KNOWN_TEST_RESULT_NUM_UNIT.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: PCT_WITH_UNIT = NUM_WITH_UNIT_N / DENOM_QUANT_KNOWN_N * 100. WITH_UNIT_FLAG=1 (EXCEPTION_FLAG=TRUE) indicates percent < 80% or numerator=0 (when denom > 0).

DESCRIPTION: More than a 5% decrease in the number of patients or records in a CDM table between the previous and current DataMart refresh
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 4.01 (Table VA): compares current vs previous snapshot of CDM tables and flags >5% decreases in record counts and distinct patient counts, or current=0 when previous>0. Previous snapshot is specified via PREV_DB_PARAM and PREV_SCHEMA_NAME passed through the driver.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('<DB_PARAM>', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '4.01', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL', '<PREV_DB_PARAM>', '<PREV_SCHEMA_NAME>'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_4_01(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL', PREV_DB_PARAM, PREV_SCHEMA_NAME).
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=4.01. CODE_TYPE indicates RECORDS or PATIENTS metrics per table.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: For each table, DECREASE_FLAG=1 indicates either a >5% drop (PCT_CHANGE < -5) or current count is 0 while previous > 0. Patient counts use the table-specific patient identifier (PATID, PROVIDERID, LAB_FACILITYID, PATID_1) when present in both snapshots.

DESCRIPTION: More than a 5% decrease in the number of patients or records for diagnosis, procedures, labs or prescriptions during an ambulatory (AV), telehealth (TH), other ambulatory (OA), emergency department (ED), or inpatient (IP) encounter between the previous and current DataMart refresh
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 4.02 (Table VB): compares current vs previous snapshot for selected encounter types (AV/ED/IP/OA/TH) and domains (diagnoses, procedures, labs, prescriptions). Flags >5% decreases in record and distinct patient counts, or current=0 when previous>0. Previous snapshot is specified via PREV_DB_PARAM and PREV_SCHEMA_NAME passed through the driver.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('<DB_PARAM>', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '4.02', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL', '<PREV_DB_PARAM>', '<PREV_SCHEMA_NAME>'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_4_02(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL', PREV_DB_PARAM, PREV_SCHEMA_NAME). TARGET_TABLE may be ALL, DIAGNOSIS, PROCEDURES, LAB_RESULT_CM, or PRESCRIBING.
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=4.02. EDC_TABLE is encounter type; SOURCE_TABLE is domain. CODE_TYPE indicates RECORDS or PATIENTS metrics per encounter type x domain.
DOC_INTERPRETATION: DECREASE_FLAG=1 indicates either a >5% drop (PCT_CHANGE < -5) or current count is 0 while previous > 0 for that encounter type and domain. ANY_DECREASE_FLAG aggregates within a cell and overall.

DESCRIPTION: More than a 5% decrease in the number of records or distinct codes for CPT/HCPCS, CVX, ICD10, LOINC, NDC, or RXNORM codes between the previous and current DataMart refresh
DOC_SUMMARY: DC 4.03 (Table VC): compares current vs previous snapshot of selected code systems and flags >5% decreases in record counts or distinct code counts, or current=0 when previous>0. Category '09' is excluded from exceptions per SAS. Previous snapshot is specified via PREV_DB_PARAM and PREV_SCHEMA_NAME passed through the driver.
DOC_RUN: Driver: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ.SP_RUN_DCQ('<DB_PARAM>', SCHEMA_NAME, 'ROW_NUM', '4.03', 'part2', TARGET_TABLE|'ALL', '<PREV_DB_PARAM>', '<PREV_SCHEMA_NAME>'). Direct: CALL CHARACTERIZATION.DCQ_CHECKS.SP_DC_4_03(DB_PARAM, SCHEMA_NAME, RUN_ID, TARGET_TABLE|'ALL', PREV_DB_PARAM, PREV_SCHEMA_NAME).
DOC_OUTPUT: Writes to <DB_PARAM>.CHARACTERIZATION_DCQ.DCQ_RESULTS where ROW_NUM=4.03. EDC_TABLE is domain; SOURCE_TABLE is the source dataset identifier (e.g., DIA_L3_DX_DXTYPE). CODE_TYPE is the code type/category (e.g., 10, CH, ND, RX, LC, CX).
DOC_INTERPRETATION: *_DECREASE_FLAG=1 indicates either a >5% drop (PCT_CHANGE < -5) or current count is 0 while previous > 0 for that domain+category. Category '09' is reported but not flagged.
